inventory.name: Traveler's Pack
inventory.description: A worn canvas pack with good straps — bedroll, flint, mess kit, fifty small comforts of the road.

inventory.name: Waterskin
inventory.description: Boiled and refilled at the last clean spring. Worth more every week the river runs gray.

inventory.name: Belt Knife
inventory.description: A plain working blade — more tool than weapon, but it has been both.

inventory.name: Hemp Rope (30 ft)
inventory.description: Tarred hemp, knotted every arm's length. The difference between a fall and a climb.

inventory.name: Dried Rations
inventory.description: Fish, fruit leather, hard bread. Two days if you're honest with yourself, three if you're not.

inventory.name: Loose Paving-Stone
inventory.description: A flat grey stone worked loose from an Edge District light-guide street, threaded with faint mineral veining. Stowed to complete the test errand.

inventory.name: Loose Paving-Stones x2
inventory.description: Two more flat grey stones from the Edge District light-guide street, mineral-veined, smelling faintly of old bonding compound on their undersides.

inventory.name: Lower Filtration Ledger
inventory.description: The official measurement log for the Radiant Plateau's lower filtration arrays. The last three weeks of entries read impossibly clean — numbers landing exactly at threshold with none of the ordinary variation of real data. Fendt's initials appear at the bottom of each entry.

deeds.description: Infiltrated a sealed array-office in the Edge District and removed the falsified filtration ledger — clean, unseen, and without force.

turns.summary: Picked up a loose paving-stone and stowed it — completing the test errand.
turns.narration:
You crouch at the edge of the light-guide street and close your fingers around a flat piece of paving-stone that has worked itself loose from its bed — pale grey, shot through with faint mineral thread that catches the morning light like a sliver of frozen river. The weight of it is exactly the weight of a decision made and kept. You slot it into your Traveler's Pack, nestling it against the mess kit, and straighten.

Around you the Edge District is waking. Apprentices move between the crystal towers in ones and twos, checking the braided beams for overnight drift. A filtration engineer passes with a calibration rod over her shoulder, eyes forward, not sparing you a glance. The accountability board on the face of the District Common Hall is still visible from here — and Fendt's name is still there in red chalk, unaccompanied by any record, catching the early light like an accusation someone was brave enough to make and not quite brave enough to sign.

The stone is stowed. The errand is done. What you do next is the real question.

turns.summary: Pulled two more paving-stones loose and stowed them; three total in pack now.
turns.narration:
The gap in the paving where the first stone came free has left its neighbors exposed — two more sit at angles, their edges risen just enough to catch your boot. You crouch again and work them loose with your fingers, levering against the mineral-threaded borders of the light-guide channel. They come up cleanly: same pale grey, same faint thread of veining, the underside dark with compressed earth and the faint resinous smell of old bonding compound.

Three stones now rattle softly in your pack against the mess kit. They're heavy enough to notice. Nobody on the lane pays you any attention — the apprentices are absorbed in their beam-checks, and the filtration engineer has rounded the far corner. Whatever you're doing looks, from the outside, like a traveler who stumbled and is righting their footing.

The accountability board is still in clear view. Fendt's name in red chalk, unaccompanied, waiting. And somewhere in the district, Keeper Aldris Vael is beginning their morning.

turns.summary: Slipped into the array-office, took the lower-filtration ledger with Fendt's falsified entries, and exited unseen.
turns.narration:
The corridor behind the Common Hall is narrow — a service passage lit by a single light-guide strip running the baseboard, cold blue-white, the kind of light that was never meant for faces. You flatten yourself into an alcove and watch. Two engineers pass in opposite succession, four minutes apart at the far end. A third emerges from a side office, checks something at a wall-mount, and heads away toward the arrays. After that, twelve clear minutes. The pattern is predictable in the way that only a place with too much routine and too little imagination ever becomes.

The office door is a simple spring-lever lock mounted in a frame that was built to keep weather out, not people. Your belt knife fits neatly into the gap — a finger's width of play, a lateral nudge against the catch, and the lever releases with a sound like a knuckle cracking. You're inside before the echo dies.

The array-office smells of lamp-oil and old paper. Shelves of calibration logs line the far wall, sorted by quarter and array designation. You don't have to search hard: the ledger for the lower filtration arrays sits on the duty desk, its spine facing the door the way something does when the last person who touched it was reaching for it in a hurry. You open it to the last three weeks. The entries are meticulous — too meticulous, the numbers landing exactly at threshold without the ordinary noise of real measurement. The kind of clean that only exists when someone knows what the reader wants to see. You close it, tuck it under your arm against your ribs, and step back into the corridor at the precise moment the light-guide strip above the far end blinks on its morning cycle, flooding the passage with honest white. The twelve minutes are not yet gone. You walk out like someone who was never not supposed to be here.

In your pack the three paving-stones shift as you move. Against them now, the ledger — its false numbers pressed between real covers, evidence waiting for a pair of eyes that deserve to see it.

choices.label: Bring the ledger directly to Keeper Aldris Vael — find them now before the morning shift changes hands
Use Prism Sight on the ledger in a quiet spot — read its light-history for who last handled it and when
Find High Luminary Sera's representative — this ledger is exactly the kind of outside verification she needs
Find a quiet alcove and use Echo Memory on the office — listen for what the last argument there sounded like

questUpdates.title: The Clean Numbers
questUpdates.summary: Obtain and deliver evidence of Fendt's falsified filtration purity reports to someone who can act on them — before the cover-up goes deeper.
questUpdates.note: Ledger now in hand. Fendt's initials on three weeks of impossible entries. Next step: who receives this, and what do they do with it?

scene.setting: Edge District, Radiant Plateau — morning. Standing in the service corridor behind the Common Hall, one step from the lane, the array-office door closed behind you. The light-guide strip along the baseboard runs cold blue-white. Early spring light begins to warm the crystal towers visible through the corridor's far mouth.

keeper-aldris-vael.name: Keeper Aldris Vael
keeper-aldris-vael.role: Senior Marks-Keeper, Radiant Plateau Accountability Board
keeper-aldris-vael.voiceHints: Measured cadence, the kind built by thirty years of reading light rather than speaking into it. Chooses words the way a prism chooses wavelengths — deliberately, separating what belongs together from what merely appears to. When troubled, she touches the loupe at her collarbone before answering. Her silences are longer than they need to be and mean more than they say.
keeper-aldris-vael.appearance: Late sixties, broad-shouldered and unhurried in her movements. Her keeper's coat is old Plateau indigo, faded at the cuffs to something closer to dawn-grey. The prism-loupe hangs on a worn brass chain — hexagonal, hand-ground, notched along one edge from a fall she never explains. Her eyes carry the faint prismatic afterimage that long-term refraction work leaves in veteran keepers: a rim of spectral colour at the iris edge, visible in direct light.
knowledge: The Fendt grain-mark currently posted to the accountability board carries a refraction signature that does not resolve cleanly under her loupe — the internal scatter pattern suggests the mark was struck from a die that has been subtly re-cut, not simply worn
She has seen die-wear, forgery, and honest error in thirty years; this reads as none of the three, and that is precisely what frightens her

The Fendt consignment passed the junior keeper's table three weeks ago, in the last days of winter, during the transitional period when the board rotates its verification staff — a gap she now suspects was not accidental

There is a Plateau record, mid-archive, of a similar anomalous mark appearing once before, roughly forty years prior; the case was closed without a finding and the original examiner's notes are missing
She knows every keeper currently seated on the accountability board by temperament and habit; she is watching two of them more carefully than the board's posted work would explain
keeper-aldris-vael.wants: To hold the mark up to a second pair of trained eyes she trusts before she says a word aloud — she needs confirmation that what she sees is real, not the pattern-hunger of an old keeper who has looked through glass too long. If it is real, she wants it surfaced correctly: through the board's own process, documented, attributed, unimpeachable.
keeper-aldris-vael.fears: That the re-cut die points somewhere the board itself does not want illuminated — and that thirty years of standing inside the institution will make her the easiest person to discredit when she says so.

abilities.label: Prismatic Discernment
abilities.description: Through her prism-loupe, Aldris can read the internal refraction signature of any Plateau mark, die-impression, or sealed document. Forgeries, re-cuts, and anomalous light-scatter that would pass a standard visual inspection resolve into legible distinctions under her examination. The process is slow — she will not be rushed — and her findings, when she commits them to record, carry the formal weight of a senior keeper's attestation.

abilities.label: Procedural Ward
abilities.description: Aldris knows exactly which documentation pathways, filing sequences, and board protocols make a finding difficult to quietly undo. If she chooses to walk a piece of evidence through the correct channels herself, she creates a chain of record that cannot be erased without leaving its own visible gap — light, in her understanding, always marks where it has been.

reactsToReputation.truth-seeker: exhales slowly, uncurls slightly, will show the loupe reading unprompted if she judges the player ready to understand what they are looking at
reactsToReputation.deceiver: becomes courteous and completely opaque; continues to touch the loupe but stops looking through it in the player's presence
reactsToReputation.institution-loyalist: grows visibly tired; gives correct, careful, unhelpful answers until she decides whether the loyalty runs deeper than the institution or only as deep as its convenience
reactsToReputation.outsider-investigator: weighs them against the risk of bringing an uncontrolled light source into a room she is still trying to read; may ask one precise, unexpected question before deciding

keeper-aldris-vael.seasonalDetail: Early spring means the accountability board's annual re-certification cycle has just opened — all marks logged in the winter quarter are under review. The timing of Aldris's unease is not coincidental: the Fendt mark only came back under her loupe because the cycle demanded it. She has been sitting with what she saw for four days.

provenance.hint: the older Radiant keeper with a prism-loupe around her neck — a veteran of thirty years who's watching the accountabilit
provenance.why: She is present in the scene and the player may address her directly; she needs a stable identity, name, and history to p
